content.full_text:
Louis N. Haddad  
5 Deer Stalker Path  
Monterey, Ca., 93940  
Phone (408) 373-5222

Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800,  
Sacramento, Ca., 95814  
August 9, 1991

Dear Members:

I was an unsuccessful candidate in last years Monterey municipal election. I am contemplating running again. I have two questions:

1. May any person circulate door-to-door more than 200 pieces of campaign literature to get around the "mailing" regulation with no notation on the literature as to who circulated it?

2. Is an elected official required to declare funds received from either the city or the official's employer for time spent away from his job?
   As an explanation, the city enacted a policy which reimburses a person who is absent from his job on city business for the time spent away, in order that the councilperson will not lose salary because he is away from his job. Either the city reimburses the employer or the funds are paid directly to the councilperson.

Thank you for your kind attention. Please answer by mail.

Sincerely,

Louis N. Haddad
